RuleSet.Name: src/schemaprotobj/IProtEditObj.java

GenFile.Name: reallyFileIProtEditObjJava
GenFile.BasePackageName: $reference ManufacturingSchema lower JavaPackage$.$reference ManufacturingSchema lower SchemaName$prot/src/main/java
GenFile.SubPackageName: $reference ManufacturingSchema lower JavaPackage$.$reference ManufacturingSchema lower SchemaName$protobj

RuleSet.GenRule: public void copyRecToOrig(); public void copyOrigToRec();
, I$reference SuperClassRelation reference ToTable SchemaName$Prot$reference SuperClassRelation reference ToTable TableName$EditObj
$switch RelationTypeTag Superclass empty Components declIProtEditObjRelationSingletonGetter Children declIProtEditObjRelationSingletonGetter default declIProtEditObjRelationSingletonAccessors$

$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIProtObjColumnBasePKeyGetter default declIProtObjColumnBaseDataGetter$$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIProtObjColumnBasePKeySetter default declIProtObjColumnBaseDataSetter$

$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIProtObjColumnBasePKeyGetter default declIProtObjColumnBaseDataGetter$$switch ColumnInPrimaryIndex yes declIProtObjColumnBasePKeySetter default declIProtObjColumnBaseDataSetter$

/* * create() may return a different instance than the * one used to invoke the operation. All future references * should be to the returned instance, not the original * invoker. You should lose all references to the original * invoker. * * @return The created instance. */ I$reference BaseClass DefSchemaName$Prot$reference BaseClass TableName$Obj create();
